title: SSC CGL T-I Reasoning Section Test

questions.question: Three of the following four numbers are alike in a certain way and one is different. Pick the odd one out.
questions.explanation: (A) 121 = 11¬≤ (B) 961 = 31¬≤ (C) 225 = 15¬≤ (D) 423 is not a perfect square Options (A), (B), and (C) are perfect squares, while option (D) is not, so it is different from the others. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: 10 is related to 102 following a certain logic. Following the same logic, 13 is related to 171. To which of the following is 16 related, following the same logic? (NOTE : Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 ‚Äì Operations on 13 such as adding / subtracting / multiplying, etc., to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)
questions.explanation: Given: '10 is related to 102' and '13 is related to 171. Logic: Square of 1st number + 2 = 2nd number 102= 100, 100 + 2 = 102 132= 169, 169 + 2 = 171 Similarly, 162= 256, 256 + 2 = 258 Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: If ' + ' means '‚Äì ', '‚Äì ' means '√ó'. '√ó' means '√∑', '√∑' means '+'+, what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation? ‚Äã38√∑10√ó5‚àí7+10√ó2=?38√∑10√ó5-7+10√ó2=?38√∑10√ó5‚àí7+10√ó2=?‚Äã‚Äã
questions.explanation: Given: 38√∑10√ó5‚àí7+10√ó2=?38√∑10√ó5-7+10√ó2=?38√∑10√ó5‚àí7+10√ó2=? ‚ÄãIf ' + ' means '‚Äì ', '‚Äì ' means 'means '√ó'. '√ó' means '√∑', '√∑' means '+', Then, Modified equation will become: ‚Äã38+10√∑5√ó7-10√∑2 38+2√ó7-5 38+14-5 52-5 47 Thus, Option C is the correct answer. ‚Äã

questions.question: Select the combination of letters that when sequentially placed in the blanks of the given series will complete theseries. _TUVTR_WU_UXVN_Y
questions.explanation: Given: _TUVTR_WU_UXVN_Y From the given options - (a) SUUP ‚ÄãSTUVTRUWUUUXVNPY (Sequence not followed) (b) SUPU ‚ÄãSTUVTRUWUPUXVNUY (Sequence followed) ‚Äã(c) SVUP ‚ÄãSTUVTRVWUUUXVNPY (Sequence not followed) (d) PSVU ‚ÄãPTUVTRSWUVUXVNUY (Sequence not followed)

questions.question: In the question given below, three statements are followed by two conclusions I and II. Read the conclusions and decide which of them logically follows. Statements: Some courts are wustice. All thefts are crimes. No crime is wustice. Conclusions: I. No court is crime. II. Some crimes are wustice.
questions.explanation: Information Given: Statements: Some courts are wustice. All thefts are crimes. No crime is wustice. Conclusions: I. No court is crime. II. Some crimes are wustice. Conclusion I: Some courts are wustice and no wustice is crime => those courts cannot be crimes We cannot say this with certainty ‚Üí does NOT follow. Conclusion II: No crime is wustice directly contradicts ‚ÄúSome crimes are wustice‚Äù ‚Üí does not follow. Final Answer: Neither I nor II follows. Final Correct Option: A

questions.question: What will come in the place of ‚Äô?‚Äô in the following equation, if ‚Äô √∑\div√∑‚Äã ‚Äô and ‚Äô√ó\times√ó‚Äã‚Äô are interchanged? ‚Äã54√ó9√∑8+22‚àí33=?54 \times 9 \div 8 + 22 - 33 = ?54√ó9√∑8+22‚àí33=?‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãOperation preference wiseSymbolBrackets[],,()Orders, of(power),‚àö(root),ofDivision√∑Multiplication√óAddition+Subtraction‚àí\begin{array}{|c|c|} \hline\textbf{Operation preference wise} & \textbf{Symbol} \\\hline\text{Brackets} &[],{}, () \\ \hline \text{Orders, of} & (power), ‚àö (root) , of \\ \hline \text{Division}& √∑ \\ \hline \text{Multiplication} & √ó \\ \hline \text{Addition} & + \\ \hline \text{Subtraction} & - \\\hline\end{array}Operation preference wiseBracketsOrders, ofDivisionMultiplicationAdditionSubtraction‚ÄãSymbol[],,()(power),‚àö(root),of√∑√ó+‚àí‚Äã‚Äã Given: ‚Äã54 √ó 9 √∑ 8 + 22 ‚àí 33 = ? Sign ‚Äã√ó Interchanged √∑ After interchanged the Sign new equation will be: 54 √∑ 9 √ó 8 + 22 ‚àí 33 = ? ? = 6 √ó 8 + 22 ‚àí 33 ? = 48 + 22 - 33 ? = 70 - 33 ? = 37 Thus, the correct option is: (b) 37‚Äã

questions.question: Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets. (NOTE : Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 ‚Äì Operations on 13 such as adding / subtracting / multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed) (3, 9, 90) (2, 5, 29)
questions.explanation: Given: (3, 9, 90) and (2, 5, 29) Logic: The third number in each set is obtained by adding the square of the first number and the square of the second number. Facts & Explanation (Operation-wise): For (3, 9, 90): 32+9 2= 9 + 81 = 90 For (2, 5, 29): 22+52= 4 + 25 = 29 Now check each option: (a) 42+12=16+1=17 (b) 72+62=49+36=85 (c) 52+172=25+289=314 (d) 52+82=25+64=89 Final Answer: (5, 8, 89) Final Correct Option: (d)

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third term on the same basis as the second term is related to the first term. 9 : 162 :: 11 : ?‚Äã ‚Äã‚Äã
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: 9 : 162 :: 11 : ? Logic: The number is coded as (number)¬≤ √ó 2. Explanation: 9¬≤ = 81, 81 √ó 2 = 162. Applying the same rule: 11¬≤ = 121, 121 √ó 2 = 242. Thus, correct option is (B).

questions.question: Which of the following pairs based on prime numbers only is similar to the expression given below? 97: 31 ‚Äã‚Äã
questions.explanation: Given: 97: 31 ‚Äã‚Äã 97 ‚Üí Prime 31 ‚Üí Prime Logic: The pair (97 : 31) consists of two prime numbers. Now check each options.‚Äã Option (a): 70 : 13 ‚Üí 70 is not prime, 13 is prime ‚ÄãOption (b): 13 : 16 ‚Üí 13 is prime, 16 is not prime Option (c): 19 : 63 ‚Üí 19 is prime, 63 is not prime Option (d): 47 : 73 ‚Üí 47 is prime, 73 is prime Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: What is the missing number '?' in the series. 24, 49, ?, 108‚Äã‚Äã
questions.explanation: Given: 24, 49, ?, 108 Logic: Double difference is + 3. The missing term is 77. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Ratnesh is Vani‚Äôs father. Laxman is Ratnesh‚Äôs father-in-law and Vani is Tanya‚Äôs sister. Aarohi is the mother of Ratnesh. Tanya is the granddaughter of Laxman. If Laxman has only one child, then how is Vani related to Laxman?
questions.explanation: Information Given: Ratnesh is Vani‚Äôs father Laxman is Ratnesh‚Äôs father-in-law Vani is Tanya‚Äôs sister Aarohi is Ratnesh‚Äôs mother Tanya is the granddaughter of Laxman Laxman has only one child ‚ÄãFinal Answer: Vani is the granddaughter of Laxman. Final Correct Option: (a) Granddaughter

questions.question: Three of the following four numbers are alike in a certain way and one is different. Pick the odd one out.
questions.explanation: Logic: All other numbers are perfect squares and also perfect cube, except 125. Check each number: 64 = 4¬≥ = 8¬≤ ‚Üí perfect cube and perfect square 1 = 1¬≥ = 1¬≤ ‚Üí perfect cube and perfect square 729 = 9¬≥ = 27¬≤ ‚Üí perfect cube and perfect square 125 = 5¬≥ ‚Üí only perfect cube ‚ùå (not a perfect square) Thus, the correct option is: (b)

questions.question: Four positions of the same dice is shown. Find the letter that will be opposite to A.
questions.explanation: Logic: If a dice has 2 face common then the last remaining face will be opposite to each other. From I and IV : B and C is common so opposite of B will be F. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: In a certain code language, ‚ÄòAll The Best‚Äô is written as ‚Äòka Na Sa‚Äô. ‚ÄòGood Better Best‚Äô is written as ‚ÄòLa Na Ma‚Äô. ‚ÄòAll Is Good‚Äô is written as ‚ÄòSa Ma Ja‚Äô How will ‚ÄòGood‚Äô be written as in that language?
questions.explanation: Given: In a certain code language, ‚ÄòAll The Best‚Äô is written as ‚Äòka Na Sa‚Äô. ‚ÄòGood Better Best‚Äô is written as ‚ÄòLa Na Ma‚Äô. ‚ÄòAll Is Good‚Äô is written as ‚ÄòSa Ma Ja‚Äô. According to given information; So, "Good" is coded as "Ma". Thus, the correct answer is (a).

questions.question: Select the option that represents the letters that, when sequentially placed from left to right in the blanks, will complete the letter series. _ M _ X J _ P X _ M P_ J_ _ X
questions.explanation: Given: _ M _ X J _ P X _ M P _ J _ _ X Now, we check each options. ‚ÄãOption (a): ‚ÄãJOJMXMP J M O X / J J P X / M M P X / J M P X ‚ÄãOption (b): ‚ÄãJPMJXMP J M P X / J M P X / J M P X / J M P X ‚Äã(It is a logical repeating pattern.)‚Äã ‚Äã‚ÄãOption (c): ‚ÄãJPMXJMP J M P X / J M P X / X M P J / J M P X ‚ÄãOption (d): ‚ÄãJPXMJMP J M P X / J X P X / M M P J / J M P X So, the following pattern is: J M P X Thus, correct option is (b). ‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: In a code language, 'DANCER' is coded as ‚ÄòREPEAD‚Äô and 'QUEAZY' is coded as ‚ÄòYZGCUQ‚Äô. How will 'SQUAWK' be coded in the same language?
questions.explanation: Given: DANCER ‚Üí REPEAD, QUEAZY ‚Üí YZGCUQ SQUAWK ‚Üí ?‚Äã ‚ÄãDANCER ‚Üí REPEAD QUEAZY ‚Üí YZGCUQ Similarly, SQUAWK ‚Üí ?‚Äã So, SQUAWK ‚Üí KWWCQS. Thus, the correct option is: (b)

questions.question: This question consists of a pair of words that have a certain relationship to each other. Select the pair that has the same relationship. (The words must be considered as meaningful English words and must not be related to each other based on the number of letters/number of consonants/vowels in the word.) Pressure : Pascal‚Äã
questions.explanation: The logic followed here is: Logic: Units of Measurement Here, Pressure : Pascal ‚Üí Pascal is the unit of measurement for Pressure. Similarly, Electric Potential : Volt ‚Üí Volt is the unit of measurement for Electric Potential. Hence, the correct answer is "Option C".
